ksa_text: Ability to design and develop secure applications using secure development methodologies, tools, and frameworks.

nist_ai_rmf.top_element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

top_anchors.element_label: Policies, processes, procedures, and practices across the organization related to the mapping, measuring, and managing of AI risks are in place, transparent, and implemented effectively.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: KSA SP-A-001 (Security and Privacy/Ability) scores 0.430 composite (highest: DDaT at 0.430) and is edge_case for the Data Engineer role. The KSA is an edge case for Security and Privacy but contains 0 DE pattern signals sufficient to warrant inclusion for a senior IC data engineer. Seniority is appropriate for IC-to-senior IC data engineering.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: No inconsistency identified. Include consistent with edge_case scope, DDaT signal (0.430), and Security and Privacy handling. Decision stands.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Does market evidence support Security/Privacy (SP) competencies for Data Engineer IC/senior IC roles?
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Data-level security (column-level security, encryption, masking, access controls) appears in 20-35% of DE job descriptions in regulated industries. Org-level policy work is absent from DE postings at IC/senior IC.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Revised to exclude. Security content is policy/org-level, not data-implementation. Not market-supported at IC/senior IC DE. Final decision: exclude.

decision_rationale: Revised to exclude. Security content is policy/org-level, not data-implementation. Not market-supported at IC/senior IC DE. Final decision: exclude.
